info.title: SendSafely REST API
info.description: The SendSafely REST API provides programmatic access to SendSafely for sending and receiving encrypted files. The API uses API key and HMAC signature-based authentication. The base URL is your SendSafely host (e.g. https://companyname.sendsafely.com or https://www.sendsafely.com) followed by /api/v2.0.

get.description: Verify that the provided API key and secret are valid.
Verify the client API version compatibility.
Retrieve information about the currently authenticated user.
Retrieve the list of dropzone recipients for the current user.

200.description: List of dropzone recipient email addresses

get.description: Retrieve the list of contact groups for the current user.

put.description: Create a new secure package for file sharing.

vdr.description: Whether to create a workspace (virtual data room)

get.description: Retrieve a list of active packages.
Retrieve a list of archived packages.
Retrieve a list of received packages.

post.description: Search packages across the organization with filters.

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific package.

post.description: Update the life (days until expiration) of a package.

life.description: Number of days until the package expires

delete.description: Delete a package that has not been finalized.

post.description: Finalize a package to make it ready for delivery. After finalization, the package link can be shared with recipients.

keyCode.description: The encryption keycode for the package

post.description: Send notification emails to all recipients of a finalized package.

put.summary: Add a recipient to a package
put.description: Add a new recipient to a package by email address.

post.description: Update a recipient's details such as phone number or role.

put.summary: Add contact group as package recipient
put.description: Add all members of a contact group as recipients of a package.

get.summary: Get public keys for a package
get.description: Retrieve the public keys associated with a package's recipients.

put.summary: Add a secure message to a package
put.description: Add an encrypted message to a package.

put.summary: Create a file placeholder in a package
put.description: Create a file ID for uploading a file to the root directory of a package.
Upload encrypted file data to a previously created file placeholder.

post.description: Get download URLs for a file in the root directory of a package.

get.description: Retrieve the contents of a directory within a package.

delete.description: Delete a directory from a package.

put.description: Create a new subdirectory within an existing directory in a package.

get.summary: Get file information from a directory
get.description: Retrieve information about a specific file within a directory.

post.description: Move a file to a different directory within a package.

parameters.name: directoryId
parameters.description: The current directory ID of the file

targetDirectoryId.description: The target directory ID to move the file to

delete.description: Delete a file from a directory within a package.

post.summary: Get file download URLs from a directory
post.description: Get download URLs for a file within a specific directory of a package.
Move a directory to a new parent directory within a package.

get.description: Retrieve the activity log for a specific package.
Retrieve the package history for a specific recipient email address.

apiKeyAuth.name: ss-api-key
apiKeyAuth.description: API key for authentication. Generate from the Edit Profile page of the SendSafely web portal.

signatureAuth.name: ss-request-signature
signatureAuth.description: HMAC-SHA256 signature computed from the API secret, URL path, timestamp, and request body.

timestampAuth.name: ss-request-timestamp
timestampAuth.description: ISO 8601 formatted timestamp of the request.
